description: VETTR AI Agent backend — pre-defined question/answer system for stock fundamentals analysis. Users select from 18 fixed questions (6 categories + 12 follow-ups) about a ticker, and the backend queries existing DB tables to generate opinionated, template-based responses with verdicts. Includes daily usage tracking with tier-based limits (FREE=3/day, PRO=15/day, PREMIUM=unlimited). No LLM — pure DB queries formatted as conversational responses.

userStories.title: Create AI agent usage tracking database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a database table to track AI agent question usage per user per day so that we can enforce tier-based daily limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/db/schema/ai-agent-usage.ts with ai_agent_usage table using pgTable

Columns: id (uuid PK, defaultRandom), user_id (uuid FK to users, notNull), question_id (varchar 50, notNull), ticker (varchar 20, notNull), asked_at (timestamp, defaultNow), date (date, notNull — for daily grouping)
Add index on (user_id, date) for fast daily count lookups
Export ai_agent_usage table and any relations from the schema file
Add export to src/db/schema/index.ts barrel file
npm run build passes with zero errors

userStories.title: Create AI agent question registry with all 18 questions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed question registry defining all 18 pre-populated questions (6 initial categories + 12 follow-ups) so that the API can validate question IDs and return available questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/ai-agent-questions.ts with typed question definitions
Define AiAgentQuestion interface: { id: string, label: string, category: string, parent_id: string | null, icon: string }

Define all 6 initial questions: financial_health ('How financially healthy is {TICKER}?'), analyst_view ('What do analysts say about {TICKER}?'), insider_activity ('Are insiders buying or selling {TICKER}?'), valuation ('Is {TICKER} fairly valued?'), earnings ('How strong are {TICKER}\'s earnings?'), red_flags ('Any red flags for {TICKER}?')

Define 12 follow-up questions (2 per category): debt_analysis, cash_position, recent_actions, price_targets, top_holders, smart_money, peer_valuation, dividend_check, earnings_beats, earnings_outlook, critical_flags, flag_trend
Export functions: getInitialQuestions(), getFollowUpQuestions(parentId: string), getQuestionById(id: string), getAllQuestions()
Each follow-up has parent_id linking to its initial question
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Implement financial health response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for financial health questions so that users get opinionated analysis of a stock's financial health, debt situation, and cash position.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/services/ai-agent-responders.ts with AiAgentResponseData interface: { summary: string, details: Array<{ label: string, value: string, status: 'safe' | 'warning' | 'danger' | 'neutral' }>, verdict: string, verdict_color: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red' }

Implement respondFinancialHealth(ticker: string) — queries financial_summary + valuation_metrics tables, returns summary with Altman Z-Score classification (Safe >2.99 / Grey 1.81-2.99 / Distress <1.81), cash runway verdict, current ratio, debt-to-equity, FCF yield with status colors
Implement respondDebtAnalysis(ticker: string) — deeper debt focus: debt-to-equity, debt-to-assets, interest coverage ratio, gross margin with contextual verdicts
Implement respondCashPosition(ticker: string) — cash runway months, operating cash flow, free cash flow, FCF yield with trend context
Each function queries DB using patterns from fundamentals.service.ts (parallel queries, n() null-safe helper)
Summary strings are opinionated: use bold markdown for key numbers, include company name, give clear assessment
All imports use .js extension (ESM requirement)
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Implement analyst consensus response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for analyst questions so that users get analysis of analyst recommendations, recent upgrades/downgrades, and price target ranges.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement respondAnalystView(ticker: string) in ai-agent-responders.ts — queries analyst_consensus + analyst_actions tables, returns consensus rating, total analysts, buy/hold/sell breakdown, price target mean, upside/downside percentage
Implement respondRecentActions(ticker: string) — last 5 analyst actions with firm names, action type (upgrade/downgrade/initiate), grade changes, dates
Implement respondPriceTargets(ticker: string) — price target mean/high/low, current price, upside %, whether consensus is above or below current price
Verdict logic: Strong Buy consensus with >20% upside = green, Hold = yellow, Sell or negative upside = red
Summary includes opinionated language: 'Wall Street is bullish on...' / 'Analysts are cautious about...' / 'Mixed signals from analysts on...'
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Implement insider activity response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for insider activity questions so that users get analysis of insider buying/selling, major holders, and smart money signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement respondInsiderActivity(ticker: string) in ai-agent-responders.ts — queries major_holders_breakdown + insider_transactions tables, returns ownership breakdown (insider/institutional/public %), net buy/sell ratio, recent transaction summary
Implement respondTopHolders(ticker: string) — institutional ownership %, insider ownership %, top holders concentration, institution count
Implement respondSmartMoney(ticker: string) — smart money signal (accumulating/neutral/distributing), institutional change %, insider transaction pattern analysis
Verdict logic: accumulating signal with net buying = green ('Smart money is flowing in'), distributing with net selling = red ('Insiders are heading for the exits'), neutral = yellow
Summary is opinionated with clear directional language
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Implement valuation response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for valuation questions so that users get analysis of whether a stock is fairly valued, how it compares to peers, and dividend sustainability.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement respondValuation(ticker: string) in ai-agent-responders.ts — queries valuation_metrics + financial_summary tables, returns P/E trailing vs forward comparison, EV/EBITDA, FCF yield, with sector average context from peer stocks

Implement respondPeerValuation(ticker: string) — queries up to 5 sector peers, compares P/E, gross margin, revenue growth, debt-to-equity across peers, identifies if target is cheap or expensive vs peers
Implement respondDividendCheck(ticker: string) — queries dividends table, returns dividend yield, estimates payout sustainability from FCF, ex-dividend date context
Verdict logic: Forward P/E < trailing P/E with growing earnings = green ('Looks undervalued'), P/E significantly above sector = red ('Premium valuation'), no dividend = neutral with note
Peer comparison summary identifies specific peers by name: '{TICKER} trades at a discount to {PEER1} and {PEER2}'
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Implement earnings quality response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for earnings questions so that users get analysis of earnings quality, beat/miss history, and forward outlook.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement respondEarnings(ticker: string) in ai-agent-responders.ts — queries earnings_history + financial_summary tables, returns overall EQ score (0-100), cash conversion ratio, accruals ratio, consecutive beats count
Implement respondEarningsBeats(ticker: string) — queries last 6 quarters of earnings history, returns beat/miss pattern with surprise percentages, identifies streak patterns
Implement respondEarningsOutlook(ticker: string) — queries earnings_estimates table, returns forward EPS estimates, expected growth vs current, revenue growth trend
Verdict logic: EQ score >70 with 3+ consecutive beats = green ('Exceptional earnings quality'), EQ <40 or consistent misses = red, mixed = yellow
Summary for beats includes specific quarters: 'Beat estimates 4 of the last 6 quarters, with an average surprise of +X%'
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Implement red flags response generators
userStories.description: As a developer, I want response generators for red flag questions so that users get analysis of risk factors, the most critical issue, and risk trend over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement respondRedFlags(ticker: string) in ai-agent-responders.ts — uses existing red-flag.service.ts detectRedFlags function, returns overall score, severity level, active flag count, top flag names and explanations
Implement respondCriticalFlags(ticker: string) — identifies the highest-severity flag, returns its full explanation, detection date, and what it means for the stock
Implement respondFlagTrend(ticker: string) — queries red_flag_history table, analyzes whether flags are increasing/decreasing, returns trend direction with context
Verdict logic: overall score >85 = red ('Critical risk level'), 60-85 = yellow ('Elevated risk'), <30 = green ('Low risk profile')
Summary for critical flags is direct: 'The biggest concern for {TICKER} is {FLAG_NAME}: {explanation}'
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Create AI agent routes with three endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want API routes for the AI agent so that the frontend can fetch available questions, submit questions, and check usage limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/routes/ai-agent.routes.ts with Hono router, apply authMiddleware to all routes
GET /ai-agent/questions — returns initial questions array; accepts optional query param ?parent_id= to return follow-up questions for that parent; response: success({ questions: AiAgentQuestion[] })

POST /ai-agent/ask — body: { question_id: string, ticker: string }, validates question_id exists via getQuestionById, validates ticker exists in stocks table, calls appropriate responder function based on question_id, returns response + follow-up questions + usage stats
GET /ai-agent/usage — returns { used: number, limit: number, remaining: number, resets_at: string } for current user's daily usage based on tier
Add export to src/routes/index.ts barrel file
Register routes in src/app.ts under /v1/ai-agent path
Use snake_case for all JSON response fields
npm run build passes

userStories.title: Add tier-based daily usage tracking and enforcement
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the AI agent to enforce daily question limits per tier so that free users are limited to 3 questions/day with an upgrade prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: In POST /ai-agent/ask route, before generating response: count today's records in ai_agent_usage for current user (WHERE user_id = ? AND date = CURRENT_DATE)
Tier limits defined as constants: FREE = 3, PRO = 15, PREMIUM = Infinity (no limit check for premium)

If daily count >= tier limit, return 429 status with error code TIER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and body: { success: false, error: { code: 'TIER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED', message: 'Daily question limit reached', details: { used, limit, upgrade_prompt: true, resets_at } } }
If within limit, insert new record into ai_agent_usage (user_id, question_id, ticker, date = CURRENT_DATE), then generate and return response
Include usage object in successful responses: { used: number, limit: number, remaining: number, resets_at: string (ISO 8601 midnight UTC next day) }
resets_at is always midnight UTC of the next day
npm run build and npm run test pass

userStories.title: Add AI agent integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want comprehensive tests for the AI agent so that I can verify all questions, responders, and usage tracking work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/__tests__/ai-agent.test.ts with vitest

Test question registry: getInitialQuestions() returns exactly 6 questions, getFollowUpQuestions('financial_health') returns exactly 2 questions, getQuestionById returns correct question or undefined for invalid ID, getAllQuestions() returns exactly 18 questions

Test responder return shape: each of the 18 responder functions returns object with { summary: string (non-empty), details: array (non-empty), verdict: string (non-empty), verdict_color: one of 'green'|'yellow'|'red' }
Test usage constants: FREE tier limit is 3, PRO is 15, PREMIUM is Infinity
Test tier limit logic: mock a function that checks count against limit, verify it returns true/false correctly for each tier at boundary values (2/3, 14/15, 999/Infinity)
All tests pass with npm run test
npm run build passes
